title: enterolobium gummiferum
intro: Enterolobium gummiferum, also known as the tamarugo tree, is a deciduous tree native to the Andes mountains in South America. The tamarugo tree has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. Its bark, leaves, and seeds have been used to treat various health issues, such as fever, diarrhea, and respiratory problems, providing health benefits like reducing inflammation and improving digestion. The herbal preparations of Enterolobium gummiferum include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made from the tree's various parts, although more research is needed to fully understand its effects.
